description: Synthetic Power BI training artifacts across 8 business domains. Each domain includes table schemas, relationships, valid measures, bad measures with expected rule triggers, visual scenarios, and modeling scenarios.

valid_measures.name: Sales YoY %
valid_measures.dax: Sales YoY % = DIVIDE([Total Sales] - [Sales PY], [Sales PY])

valid_measures.name: Avg Order Value
valid_measures.dax: Avg Order Value = DIVIDE([Total Sales], DISTINCTCOUNT(FactSales[SalesID]))

valid_measures.name: Sales Rolling 3M
valid_measures.dax: Sales Rolling 3M = CALCULATE([Total Sales], DATESINPERIOD(DimDate[Date], LASTDATE(DimDate[Date]), -3, MONTH))

valid_measures.name: Top 10 Customers Sales
valid_measures.dax: Top 10 Customers Sales = CALCULATE([Total Sales], TOPN(10, ALL(DimCustomer), [Total Sales], DESC))

bad_measures.name: Sales Current Year Bad
bad_measures.dax: Sales Current Year Bad = CALCULATE([Total Sales], FILTER(ALL(DimDate), DimDate[Year] = YEAR(TODAY())))
bad_measures.issue: FILTER(ALL(dim)) for single equality — should use direct column filter

bad_measures.name: Sales Category Bad
bad_measures.dax: Sales Category Bad = CALCULATE([Total Sales], FILTER(ALL(DimProduct), DimProduct[Category] = SELECTEDVALUE(DimProduct[Category])))
bad_measures.issue: SELECTEDVALUE without ISFILTERED guard — circular filter risk

bad_measures.name: Sales Rank Bad
bad_measures.dax: Sales Rank Bad = RANKX(ALL(FactSales), [Total Sales])
bad_measures.issue: RANKX over entire fact table — should rank over dimension

bad_measures.name: Formatted Sales Bad
bad_measures.dax: Formatted Sales Bad = FORMAT([Total Sales], "$#,##0.00")
bad_measures.issue: FORMAT in aggregated measure returns text, not number

bad_measures.name: Sales This Year Bad
bad_measures.dax: Sales This Year Bad = CALCULATE([Total Sales], DimDate[Year] = YEAR(TODAY()))
bad_measures.issue: YEAR(TODAY()) in measure recalculates at query time — use a slicer or parameter

visual_scenarios.issue: MonthName sorts alphabetically, not by month number
visual_scenarios.fix: Sort MonthName by Month column in DimDate

visual_scenarios.issue: Gross Margin showing as sum across categories instead of weighted average
visual_scenarios.fix: Gross Margin should use SUM(Sales) - SUM(Cost) / SUM(Sales), not AVERAGE(Margin%)

visual_scenarios.issue: Card shows blank when no date filter is active
visual_scenarios.fix: Add ISBLANK guard or use MAX(DimDate[Date]) as anchor for DATESYTD

modeling_scenarios.issue: DimTerritory has bidirectional filter to FactSales — RLS on TerritoryKey leaks through to other dimensions
modeling_scenarios.fix: Set to single-directional; use CROSSFILTER in measures that need reverse filtering

modeling_scenarios.scenario: FactSales used as dimension in report
modeling_scenarios.issue: SalesID placed on row axis of matrix — 5M rows returned to visual
modeling_scenarios.fix: Create DimOrder dimension or use aggregated measure in visual

tables.name: FactGL
tables.grain: one row per general ledger posting

tables.name: DimAccount
tables.grain: one row per chart of accounts entry

tables.name: DimBudget
tables.grain: one row per account/cost center/month budget entry

bad_measures.name: Revenue Hardcoded Year Bad
bad_measures.dax: Revenue 2024 = CALCULATE([Revenue], DimDate[Year] = 2024)
bad_measures.issue: Hardcoded year literal — will be wrong next year

bad_measures.name: Department Summary Bad
bad_measures.dax: Dept Summary Bad = SUMMARIZE(FactGL, DimCostCenter[Division], "Total", [Actual Amount])
bad_measures.issue: SUMMARIZE with measures — use SUMMARIZECOLUMNS instead

bad_measures.name: FX Converted Bad
bad_measures.dax: USD Amount Bad = SUMX(FactGL, FactGL[Amount] * LOOKUPVALUE(DimExchange[Rate], DimExchange[Currency], FactGL[Currency]))
bad_measures.issue: LOOKUPVALUE inside SUMX when a relationship could be used

visual_scenarios.issue: Parent header accounts included in waterfall causing double-counting
visual_scenarios.fix: Filter IsHeader = FALSE in visual or measure

modeling_scenarios.issue: DimBudget contains both budget rows and is related to DimAccount — many-to-many fan trap possible
modeling_scenarios.fix: Create a bridge table or use TREATAS to apply budget context

tables.name: FactSubscriptions
tables.grain: one row per subscription per month

tables.name: FactUsage
tables.grain: one row per account per feature per day

valid_measures.name: Churn Rate
valid_measures.dax: Churn Rate = DIVIDE(CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactSubscriptions), FactSubscriptions[Status] = "Churned"), CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactSubscriptions), FactSubscriptions[Status] IN {"Active", "Churned"}))

valid_measures.name: Feature Adoption Rate
valid_measures.purpose: Percentage of active accounts using a feature

bad_measures.name: MRR Trend Bad
bad_measures.dax: MRR Trend Bad = CALCULATE([MRR], DATEADD(DimDate[Date], -365, DAY))
bad_measures.issue: DATEADD with -365 days instead of SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR — misses leap years, slower

bad_measures.name: Churn Accounts Bad
bad_measures.dax: Churn Accounts Bad = COUNTROWS(FILTER(FactSubscriptions, FactSubscriptions[Status] = "Churned"))
bad_measures.issue: COUNTROWS(FILTER(fact, condition)) — use CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(), condition) instead

bad_measures.name: Top Plan Revenue Bad
bad_measures.dax: Top Plan Revenue Bad = CALCULATE([MRR], TOPN(3, ALL(DimPlan), [MRR], DESC))
bad_measures.issue: TOPN without tiebreaker — plans with equal MRR have nondeterministic inclusion

visual_scenarios.issue: ARR is 12x MRR — both on same axis makes MRR line invisible at scale
visual_scenarios.fix: Use secondary axis for ARR or show separately

visual_scenarios.issue: Funnel requires ordered sequential stages — AcquisitionChannel is categorical
visual_scenarios.fix: Use bar chart for channel comparison; use funnel only for conversion stages

modeling_scenarios.scenario: FactUsage and FactSubscriptions both connected to DimAccount
modeling_scenarios.issue: Chained fact tables with shared dimension — cross-filtering may produce unexpected BLANK rows
modeling_scenarios.fix: Use TREATAS to apply account context across fact tables explicitly

tables.name: FactInventory
tables.grain: one row per product per warehouse per day (snapshot)

tables.name: FactPurchaseOrders
tables.grain: one row per purchase order line

valid_measures.name: Stock Out Count
valid_measures.purpose: Number of product-warehouse combos with zero available stock

valid_measures.name: Days of Supply
valid_measures.dax: Days of Supply = DIVIDE([Total On Hand], DIVIDE([Total Sales], 30))
valid_measures.purpose: Days of inventory remaining at current sell-through rate

bad_measures.name: Inventory Text Value Bad
bad_measures.dax: Inventory Text Bad = SUMX(FactInventory, FactInventory[OnHandQty] * FORMAT(FactInventory[UnitCost], "$#.00"))
bad_measures.issue: FORMAT inside SUMX converts to text — multiplication fails or causes type coercion

bad_measures.name: Stock Search Bad
bad_measures.dax: Product Search Bad = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactInventory), SEARCH("widget", DimProduct[ProductName], 1, 0) > 0)
bad_measures.issue: SEARCH used where FIND would suffice for case-insensitive search; both are row-context evaluated

visual_scenarios.issue: Scatter with 2000 product points — too dense to read
visual_scenarios.fix: Aggregate to Category level or add a Top N filter

modeling_scenarios.scenario: Snapshot fact table summed without date filter
modeling_scenarios.issue: FactInventory is a periodic snapshot — summing OnHandQty without date filter multiplies values across all days
modeling_scenarios.fix: Always filter FactInventory to LASTDATE or a specific snapshot date in measures

valid_measures.name: On Time Delivery Rate
valid_measures.dax: On Time Delivery Rate = DIVIDE(CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactShipments), FactShipments[ActualDeliveryDate] <= FactShipments[PromisedDeliveryDate]), COUNTROWS(FactShipments))
valid_measures.purpose: Percentage of shipments delivered on or before promised date

valid_measures.name: Shipments by Destination
valid_measures.purpose: Count filtered by destination using inactive relationship

bad_measures.name: Late Days Bad
bad_measures.dax: Late Days Bad = COUNTROWS(ADDCOLUMNS(FactShipments, "Late", FactShipments[ActualDeliveryDate] - FactShipments[PromisedDeliveryDate]))
bad_measures.issue: COUNTROWS(ADDCOLUMNS(fact)) — ADDCOLUMNS iterates all rows unnecessarily; use CALCULATE(COUNTROWS()) with filter

bad_measures.name: Carrier List Bad
bad_measures.dax: Carrier List Bad = CONCATENATEX(DimCarrier, DimCarrier[CarrierName], ", ")
bad_measures.issue: CONCATENATEX without ORDERBY — carrier order is nondeterministic

visual_scenarios.issue: City names are ambiguous (Springfield appears in 35 US states) — map geocodes incorrectly
visual_scenarios.fix: Use latitude/longitude columns or concatenate City + Country for geocoding

modeling_scenarios.scenario: Role-playing DimLocation with inactive relationship for destination
modeling_scenarios.issue: Only one relationship can be active — destination analysis requires USERELATIONSHIP in every measure
modeling_scenarios.fix: Create DimOrigin and DimDestination as separate views or calculated tables from DimLocation

tables.name: FactHeadcount
tables.grain: one row per employee per month (snapshot)

valid_measures.name: Attrition Rate
valid_measures.dax: Attrition Rate = DIVIDE(CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactHeadcount), FactHeadcount[Status] = "Terminated"), CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactHeadcount), PREVIOUSMONTH(DimDate[Date]), FactHeadcount[Status] = "Active"))

valid_measures.name: Avg Tenure Years
valid_measures.dax: Avg Tenure Years = AVERAGEX(FILTER(FactHeadcount, FactHeadcount[Status] = "Active"), DATEDIFF(FactHeadcount[HireDate], TODAY(), YEAR))
valid_measures.purpose: Average years of service for active employees

bad_measures.name: Manager List Bad
bad_measures.dax: Manager List = CONCATENATEX(FILTER(DimEmployee, DimEmployee[IsManager] = TRUE()), DimEmployee[FullName], ", ")
bad_measures.issue: CONCATENATEX without ORDERBY — names appear in nondeterministic order

bad_measures.name: Salary Range Bad
bad_measures.dax: Over Band = COUNTROWS(FILTER(FactHeadcount, FactHeadcount[Salary] > RELATED(DimJobLevel[SalaryMax])))
bad_measures.issue: RELATED inside FILTER in measure context — RELATED only works in row context of the same table

bad_measures.name: User RLS Bad
bad_measures.dax: My Dept Headcount = CALCULATE([Headcount], FILTER(DimDepartment, DimEmployee[ManagerKey] = LOOKUPVALUE(DimEmployee[EmployeeKey], DimEmployee[FullName], USERNAME())))
bad_measures.issue: USERNAME() deprecated in Power BI Service — use USERPRINCIPALNAME()

visual_scenarios.issue: Power BI does not have a native org chart visual — decomposition tree or third-party visual needed
visual_scenarios.fix: Use Decomposition Tree for hierarchy exploration or import AppSource org chart visual

modeling_scenarios.issue: Summing salary across all months in the snapshot gives inflated total — salary is a semi-additive measure
modeling_scenarios.fix: Use LASTNONBLANK or filter to single month when calculating salary totals

tables.name: FactCampaigns
tables.grain: one row per campaign per day

bad_measures.name: Campaign Rank Bad
bad_measures.dax: Campaign Rank Bad = RANKX(ALL(FactCampaigns), [ROAS])
bad_measures.issue: RANKX over fact table — should rank over DimChannel or aggregated table

bad_measures.name: Channel Spend Formatted Bad
bad_measures.dax: Channel Spend Display = FORMAT(SUM(FactCampaigns[Spend]), "$#,##0")
bad_measures.issue: FORMAT in measure returns text — cannot use in calculations or sort numerically

bad_measures.name: Multi OR Filter Bad
bad_measures.dax: Social Spend Bad = CALCULATE([CTR], DimChannel[Platform] = "Facebook" || DimChannel[Platform] = "Instagram" || DimChannel[Platform] = "Twitter" || DimChannel[Platform] = "TikTok")
bad_measures.issue: Multiple OR conditions — use IN operator or VALUES() for better readability and potential optimization

visual_scenarios.issue: Donut with 30 channels — too many slices, chart unreadable
visual_scenarios.fix: Limit to top 5-7 channels, group remainder as 'Other'

modeling_scenarios.scenario: FactCampaigns and FactWebAnalytics both connected to DimChannel
modeling_scenarios.issue: When filtering by channel, both fact tables filter — but campaign spend and web sessions may not have 1:1 granularity causing misaligned aggregations
modeling_scenarios.fix: Separate page-level visuals for campaign vs web metrics; avoid mixing fact tables in single visual

valid_measures.name: Maintenance Cost per Asset
valid_measures.dax: Maintenance Cost per Asset = DIVIDE(SUM(FactWorkOrders[Cost]), DISTINCTCOUNT(FactWorkOrders[AssetKey]))

bad_measures.name: Asset Age Calc Column Bad
bad_measures.dax: Asset Age Years = DATEDIFF(DimAsset[InstalledDate], TODAY(), YEAR)
bad_measures.issue: TODAY() in calculated column — evaluated at refresh time, not at query time; stale between refreshes

bad_measures.name: Efficiency Text Bad
bad_measures.dax: Efficiency Display Bad = CONCATENATEX(DimTechnician, DimTechnician[TechnicianName] & ": " & FORMAT([OEE], "0%"), UNICHAR(10))
bad_measures.issue: CONCATENATEX without ORDERBY and FORMAT in DAX context — nondeterministic order plus text type

bad_measures.name: Work Type Switch Bad
bad_measures.dax: Work Category Bad = SWITCH(FactWorkOrders[WorkType], "PM", "Preventive", "CM", "Corrective", "PdM", "Predictive", "EM", "Emergency", "EM", "Emergency Duplicate", "Unknown")
bad_measures.issue: SWITCH with duplicate 'EM' condition — second EM branch is unreachable

visual_scenarios.issue: Gauge target set to absolute value 0.85 but OEE can exceed 1.0 in some definitions — scale misleading
visual_scenarios.fix: Use a KPI visual with status thresholds or card with conditional formatting

modeling_scenarios.issue: Two date foreign keys — only StartDateKey has an active relationship; filtering by month misses events that started in prior month
modeling_scenarios.fix: Use USERELATIONSHIP in measures for EndDateKey; or expand downtime into a daily snapshot table in Power Query

DimDate.note: All domains share this date dimension. Always mark as a date table. Ensure Date column has no gaps.

DimProduct.note: Shared between sales and inventory domains. ProductKey must be consistent.

power_query_scenarios.code_pattern: List.Generate(() => StartDate, each _ <= EndDate, each Date.AddDays(_, 1))
power_query_scenarios.issue: List.Generate breaks query folding — executes in Power Query engine instead of being pushed to source
power_query_scenarios.fix: Use Table.AddColumn with Date arithmetic or use a SQL-side calendar table

power_query_scenarios.issue: Table.Buffer forces evaluation in memory and breaks folding for all downstream steps
power_query_scenarios.fix: Remove Table.Buffer unless specifically needed to prevent re-evaluation of a random or time-sensitive source

power_query_scenarios.issue: Silently converts errors to null — hides data quality issues; nulls propagate silently through calculations
power_query_scenarios.fix: Use explicit type checks or Table.SelectRows to filter out bad rows; log errors separately

power_query_scenarios.issue: Parameter without explicit type — Power Query may infer wrong type, causing date arithmetic failures
power_query_scenarios.fix: Declare parameter with explicit type: Date.From(StartDate)

power_query_scenarios.scenario: Excessive steps (>20) in a single query
power_query_scenarios.issue: More than 20 steps in one query makes the query hard to debug, slows the editor, and increases refresh memory usage
power_query_scenarios.fix: Split into staging queries: source → cleaned → transformed → final. Reference staging queries.
